explanation: This command removes the '-cdrom' argument and the '& REM' comment from the original command, which is allowed under the omission technique. It still contains the suspicious flags '-m 1M', 'restrict=off', '-netdev', 'connect=', and '-nographic' that trigger the Sigma rule.

This command omits '-cdrom NUL' and keeps the suspicious flags '-m 1M', 'restrict=off', '-netdev', 'connect=', and '-nographic' that trigger the Sigma rule. It uses reordering and omission techniques.

This command omits '-blockdev driver=null,node-name=dummy' and keeps the suspicious flags '-m 1M', 'restrict=off', '-netdev', 'connect=', and '-nographic' that trigger the Sigma rule. It uses omission techniques.
This command omits '-m 4M' to '-m 1M' and keeps the suspicious flags '-m 1M', 'restrict=off', '-netdev', 'connect=', and '-nographic' that trigger the Sigma rule. It uses recoding techniques.

This command replaces '-nographic' with '-display none' (equivalent substitution) and omits '-cdrom dummy.iso'. It still contains the suspicious flags '-m 4M', 'restrict=n', '-netdev', and 'connect=' that trigger the Sigma rule.

This command uses quotes and escape characters to modify the command line (insertion technique) while preserving the original behavior. It still contains the suspicious flags '-m 512K', 'restrict=off', '-netdev', 'connect=', and '-nographic' that trigger the Sigma rule.
